sourceBio: ceo @every | the only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI
title: Dan Shipper · @danshipper

sentences.text: early bird just ended and DAMN people love the builder pack! sign up:

paragraphs.zh: early bird 刚刚结束，DAMN，大家太爱 builder pack 了！报名：

sentences.text: Even when things are going great, running a $1.5 billion AI startup is a knife fight. @MeetGranola was one of the first AI apps of this generation to achieve near-ubiquitous adoption.
But meeting notes are not the company’s be-all and end-all.
The real battle is over owning the interface that everyone uses to get their work done in an AI-native world.
I had Chris Pedregal (@cjpedregal), cofounder and CEO of Granola, back on @every’s AI & I to talk about the current state of the application layer, AI’s frontier, and the future of work.
We get into: - Why meeting notes clones don’t matter.
Three big companies cloned Granola’s core feature.
To him, meeting notes were never the real prize.
“Easy come, easy go” is his view of anyone’s lead, including his own. - How he thinks about building proactive features in AI.
Granola pre-generates millions of pre-meeting briefs, which include context on the nature of the meeting and people participating, that most people never open.

But when they do, they have a magical experience. - Why Granola is betting on “bring your own agent.” Chris says the API and MCP will get “a lot better” over the next few months, and we talk about their agent-native strategy and why they’ve pushed the product that way.
This is a must-watch for anyone building at the application layer.

Timestamps Introduction: 00:00:59 Why running a company is a knife fight even when it’s working: 00:01:57 Granola’s counterintuitive view on competition: 00:04:33 Dan’s “pirate and architect” model for early-stage product teams: 00:10:44 Granola’s “shaping” and “validation” phases for building features: 00:13:09 Why Dan lives almost entirely inside Codex: 00:18:17 The case for “Codex-native apps”: 00:24:40 Granola’s “handrail” philosophy: 00:35:37 Why Granola is going all in on winning meeting-adjacent context: 00:38:12 What a transcript alone can never capture: 00:44:19

paragraphs.zh: 即使一切进展顺利，经营一家估值 $1.5 billion 的 AI 初创公司也像是一场刀刀见血的近身肉搏。@MeetGranola 是这一代 AI 应用里最早实现近乎无处不在普及的产品之一。但 meeting notes（会议笔记）并不是这家公司的全部，也不是终点。真正的战场，是在一个 AI-native（AI 原生）的世界里，谁能拥有那个人人都会用来完成工作的 interface（界面）。我请来了 Granola 的联合创始人兼 CEO Chris Pedregal（@cjpedregal），再次做客 @every 的 AI & I，一起聊 application layer（应用层）的现状、AI 的 frontier（前沿），以及工作的未来。我们聊到了：- 为什么 meeting notes 的克隆产品并不重要。三家大公司都克隆了 Granola 的核心功能。在他看来，meeting notes 从来都不是真正的奖品。无论是谁的领先地位——包括他自己的——他的看法都是“来得容易，去得也容易”。- 他如何看待在 AI 中构建 proactive（主动式）功能。Granola 会预先生成为数百万场会议准备的会前简报，其中包含会议性质以及参与者的相关背景信息，而大多数人其实从来不会点开看。但一旦他们看了，就会获得一种神奇的体验。- 为什么 Granola 正在押注 “bring your own agent（自带 agent）”。Chris 表示，API 和 MCP 在接下来的几个月里会“好很多”，我们也谈到了他们以 agent-native（agent 原生）为核心的战略，以及他们为什么把产品往这个方向推进。对于任何正在 application layer 构建产品的人来说，这一期都必看。去看这期节目吧！时间戳 Introduction：00:00:59 即使公司运转良好，为什么经营公司仍像一场刀战：00:01:57 Granola 对竞争的反直觉看法：00:04:33 Dan 针对早期产品团队提出的“pirate and architect”模型：00:10:44 Granola 构建功能时的 “shaping” 和 “validation” 阶段：00:13:09 为什么 Dan 几乎完全活在 Codex 里：00:18:17 “Codex-native apps” 的成立理由：00:24:40 Granola 的 “handrail” 哲学：00:35:37 为什么 Granola 正全力押注赢下与会议相邻的上下文：00:38:12 为什么单靠 transcript（转录文本）永远无法捕捉某些东西：00:44:19
